2605.03537.title: A Skill-Based AI Agentic Pipeline for Library of Congress Subject Indexing
2605.03537.paper_summary: This paper presents a modular AI agentic skill pipeline for automating subject indexing with Library of Congress Subject Headings (LCSH). Subject indexing - the process of analyzing a work's aboutness, selecting controlled vocabulary terms, and encoding them as MARC21 subject access fields - is one of the most time-consuming components of library cataloging. The system decomposes this process into four discrete, sequentially executed agent skills: conceptual analysis, quantitative filtering, authority validation, and MARC field synthesis. Each skill encodes domain knowledge drawn directly from Library of Congress Subject Headings Manual (SHM) instruction sheets and subject analysis theory. The pipeline was evaluated against a corpus of ten titles whose existing subject headings were captured from the Harvard Library bibliographic dataset (a snapshot of their Alma ILS). Results demonstrate strong conceptual alignment with professional subject indexing practice, with notable differences in specificity, subdivision practice, and the agent's adherence to the 2026 LC policy discontinuing form subdivisions in favor of LCGFT 655 fields.
2605.03537.paper_summary_zh: 本論文提出了一個模組化的人工智慧代理技能管道，用於自動化使用國會圖書館主題標題（LCSH）進行主題索引。主題索引——分析作品的主題性、選擇受控詞彙術語並將其編碼為MARC21主題存取欄位的過程——是圖書館編目中最耗時的組成部分之一。該系統將此過程分解為四個獨立的、按順序執行的代理技能：概念分析、定量過濾、權威驗證和MARC欄位合成。每個技能都編碼了直接來自國會圖書館主題標題手冊（SHM）指導文件和主題分析理論的領域知識。該管道的評估對象是十個標題的語料庫，其現有的主題標題來自哈佛圖書館的書目數據集（他們的Alma ILS快照）。結果顯示出與專業主題索引實踐之間的強概念對齊，並在具體性、細分實踐以及代理對2026年國會圖書館政策的遵循（該政策停止使用形式細分，轉而使用LCGFT 655欄位）方面存在顯著差異。
2605.03537.author: Eric H. C. Chow et.al.
